Empires & Puzzles Garnet Review: The Ninja of Soaring Vitality

Remember when ailment immunity was a luxury? Garnet remembers. In this review, we revisit the Ninja of Soaring Vitality to see if her flexible mana charge and preventive medicine can still save your team from the nasty status effects of the modern era, or if her stats are just too old school for the new meta.
Garnet, The Ninja of Vitality: Soaring Health and Preventive Care

Garnet is a 5-star Legendary Fire hero from the Ninja Tower event. When she first arrived on the scene in late 2020, she revolutionized the healer role with the Ninja family's signature "Charge" mana speed. Unlike traditional healers who operate on a fixed schedule, Garnet offers flexibility: a quick immunization shot, a standard heal, or a massive health overhaul. As a Sorcerer, she also has a chance to delay enemy mana generation, though her primary job is keeping your team alive and, more importantly, clean of status ailments.

Garnet sports a classic ninja aesthetic with a vibrant crimson color palette that matches her element perfectly. She wields a pair of sais and wears a mask that screams "social distancing champion." Her dynamic pose suggests she is ready to leap into action—or perhaps she is just dodging all the responsibilities of being a healer in a game where everyone hits for 1000 damage now. It is a sleek, focused design that fits the "silent but deadly" (or in her case, "silent but healthy") theme of the Ninja family.

To put it bluntly, Garnet's stats are showing their age. With a base Power of 798, she is significantly behind the curve when compared to modern heroes who regularly push past 1260 Power. Her Attack (731) and Defense (751) are hundreds of points lower than recent releases like Jwala or Telonius. While her Health (1481) was once impressive, it is now eclipsed by 4-star heroes. In the current meta, she is fragile. If you plan to use her, she will rely heavily on Emblems, Limit Breaks, and perhaps a bodyguard to survive the initial volley of attacks.
, is the definition of versatility. The core mechanic is the Charge system, allowing you to fire at x1, x2, or x3 mana.

A quick 230 boosted health and 2 turns of ailment immunity. This is her bread and butter in fast-paced raids. It counters heavy ailment casters like Alfrike or the new goblin dot-dealers before they can ruin your day.
A solid 450 boosted health and 3 turns of immunity. This is comparable to an average speed healer but with the added benefit of boosting health above max.
A massive 750 boosted health and 4 turns of immunity. While powerful, you will rarely reach this in a competitive match unless the board is incredibly generous or you are playing very passively.

At x1, 230 HP is a "top-up," roughly 10-15% of a modern hero's health.
At x2, 450 HP is a standard heal, about 20-25%.
At x3, 750 HP is a major reversal, potentially 35-40% of health.

Family Bonus, Passives, Aether Power & Unique Abilities
The Ninja Family bonus is one of the best in the game. It provides a chance to

. Even with just two unique Ninja heroes, you get a 5% chance to dodge and counter. While the percentage seems low, when it triggers, it can completely swing a match by negating a sniper hit or a massive AoE. It adds a layer of survivability that Garnet desperately needs given her older stats.

Garnet does not possess modern passive skills like "start battle with mana" or "resist mana reduction," which puts her at a tactical disadvantage against newer heroes who come loaded with extra abilities.

is an odd choice for a pure support healer. It boosts her attack at the start of the battle, which helps with tile damage, but offers zero defensive utility or healing amplification. Ideally, a healer would want Defense Up, Ailment Immunity, or Regeneration. This Aether Power feels like a mismatch and does not synergize well with her primary role.

Upon release, Garnet was a meta-defining hero. She was arguably the best healer in the game, replacing staples like Grazul and creating a "red wall" of immunity that frustrated ailment-heavy defenses. She was a top-tier tank and flank in wars.

The game has evolved drastically. Damage dealers now hit for 3-4 times the damage they did in 2020. Garnet's heal values, which are fixed numbers rather than percentages, have effectively shrunk relative to the massive HP pools of modern heroes. 230 health is a drop in the bucket for a hero with 3000 HP.
, but niche. She is no longer a top-tier defensive hero because she is too easy to kill. However, on

, she remains a tactical nuke against ailment teams. Being able to block Alfrike, Kullervo, or potent DoT heroes with just 5 tiles is a utility that never goes out of style. She has moved from a "must-have everywhere" to a "specialist tool" for veteran players.
Garnet needs to survive. Her base defense and health are too low for the current era, so every emblem node must be dedicated to keeping her alive long enough to fire.

path is the only logical choice. Her healing is a fixed amount, so increasing her Attack stat does not improve her healing output. Her only job is to live and cast Immunity; dead doctors save no patients.
Prevention is better than cure. Blocking effects like Mindless Attack or Mana Steal is invaluable.
The option to choose between a quick cleanse-block or a big heal allows for high-level tactical play.
Overhealing is a powerful mechanic that counters Max Health reduction and keeps teams alive through attrition.
The dodge chance is a "get out of jail free" card that can randomly save a match.
She is incredibly squishy by modern standards and can be one-shot by current snipers.
Her healing values have not scaled with the massive health pools of newer heroes.

Her boosted health and immunity are critical for surviving the curse mechanics and long battles.
Boosted health allows your team to enter boss waves with double HP, making hard modes significantly easier.
While she is fast, her x3 charge becomes Very Fast in Rush wars, making her an absolute monster who provides massive overheal and 4 turns of immunity constantly.
The key to using Garnet today is protection. You cannot leave her exposed.
Pair her with a taunt hero. The taunt forces enemies to attack the tank, buying Garnet time to charge her x2 or x3 skills safely.
Run her with a cleanser (like Rigard or costumed Ariel). Garnet prevents new ailments, while the cleanser removes any that slipped through before she fired.
Pairing her with another Ninja increases the family dodge chance. This turns your entire team into a slippery target while she keeps them healthy.
(ironically) can bypass her buffs. Diswellers like
or any heavy hitter can simply delete her before she charges due to her low stats.
Avoid pairing her with other heroes who provide "Ailment Immunity" (like Vanda or Grazul) as their effects overwrite each other rather than stacking, leading to wasted utility.

If you pulled Garnet, you have struck gold for your PvE progression. She will carry you through the map and quests by boosting your team's health beyond its maximum. Prioritize leveling her immediately. In raids, practice using her x1 charge; do not get greedy waiting for x3 unless you are safe.

Garnet is a "break in case of emergency" hero. You likely have healers with better stats, but do you have a 5-tile ailment blocker? Probably not many. She is worth keeping on your roster for specific war counters against Alfrike or heavy DoT teams. However, think twice before using Alpha Aethers on her unless you simply have no other ailment blockers; her stats may not justify the premium resource compared to a modern powerhouse.
Back in the day, the community was buzzing about the Ninja mechanic.

"How does Garnet’s Special Skill... match Garnet’s Base Stats? ...Better than Most."

Garnet is like a classic car: stylish, reliable mechanics, and built with a clear purpose, but she lacks the horsepower (stats) of the modern supercars. She is the "Original Immunization Station," and while the clinic might be a bit run-down compared to the new hospitals, the vaccine she offers is just as effective. Use her wisely, protect her, and she will keep your team healthy and ailment-free. Just do not ask her to tank a hit from a 2026 hero.
Still a valuable strategic asset for her x1 charge immunity, but requires careful handling due to aged stats. Not a primary defender anymore, but an offensive ace in the hole.
